text: Debugging is a crucial skill for any programmer. When your code doesn’t work as expected, the first step is identifying the bug. A systematic approach helps you locate the problem efficiently. Instead of guessing, isolate the issue by testing different parts of your code piece by piece. Many debugging tools are available, such as breakpoints in your code editor, which pause the program at a specific line so you can inspect variable values. Additionally, logging is extremely useful. By adding print statements or using a logging library, you can track the flow of your program and see intermediate results. Together, these strategies make debugging less frustrating and more productive.
